dailyRoutine.title: Ragnarok Origin Classic Daily Routine
dailyRoutine.subtitle: A clean daily loop helps players keep leveling, farming, and unlocking systems without wasting limited combat time.
dailyRoutine.intro: In Ragnarok Origin Classic, the fastest progress comes from doing the right things in the right order instead of grinding randomly. A good daily route usually starts with free rewards and story progress, then shifts into daily commissions, combat-time farming, bosses, and guild content.

items.name: Claim daily rewards first
items.details: Collect daily rewards, event rewards, and battle pass freebies before starting long sessions.
items.why_it_matters: This is the easiest source of early tickets, consumables, and progression materials.

items.name: Push the main story until level-gated
items.details: Main quests remain the main source of early EXP, Job EXP, feature unlocks, and basic gear progression.
items.why_it_matters: Story progression unlocks systems faster than random field grinding.

items.name: Finish daily commissions
items.details: Daily commissions are quick repeatable quests that usually ask players to defeat monsters or complete small objectives.
items.why_it_matters: They provide steady EXP, Zeny, and useful materials while fitting naturally into the daily loop.

items.name: Spend combat time efficiently
items.details: Use combat time on monsters you can kill quickly, ideally targets near your level or mobs tied to needed drops.
items.why_it_matters: Combat time is one of the core efficiency systems for leveling, farming, and drop generation.

items.name: Do daily activities and rotating event tasks
items.details: Regular daily activities and limited events add extra growth materials, bonus rewards, and seasonal progression.
items.why_it_matters: These activities stack account value over time and are often the easiest way to keep up as F2P.

items.name: Hunt Mini and MVP bosses when possible
items.details: Mini and MVP boss content is tied to valuable drops such as rare materials, cards, and blueprints.
items.why_it_matters: Boss content is one of the main ways to convert routine play into long-term gear progression.

items.name: Join guild activities
items.details: Guild quests and guild events add contribution rewards and extra account growth on top of solo play.
items.why_it_matters: Guild participation is a repeatable source of progression that many new players skip too often.

items.name: Use the Handbook before free farming
items.details: The Handbook helps players check upgrade materials, monster locations, and better farming targets.
items.why_it_matters: It prevents wasted time when players hit story walls and need to pivot into efficient farming.

highlights: Daily Commissions, combat time, and rewards should stay at the top of the loop.
Guild tasks and boss hunts add value that compounds over time.
The Handbook is the fastest way to recover when story progress slows down.

skillBuilds.title: Ragnarok Origin Classic Skill Builds
skillBuilds.subtitle: Skill-build traffic is highly practical because players usually search after choosing a class and wanting a clear direction.
skillBuilds.intro: Ragnarok Origin Classic build discussions online are already converging around recognizable skill packages rather than one universal point-spread for every player. The most stable homepage format is to show each advanced class with its main skill route, what the route is for, and the core tradeoff between farming, bossing, PvP, and budget friendliness.

items.best_for: Early to midgame farming, steady ranged DPS, general PvE
items.notes: Online build coverage for Sniper consistently highlights ADL and Falcon-oriented setups as one of the most common ranged DPS routes.
Trapper is commonly presented as the alternative to ADL for players who prefer setup damage instead of constant auto attacks.
Current build coverage repeatedly separates Lord Knight between Pierce, Clashing Spiral, Bash, and Crit-based directions.

items.best_for: AoE grinding, party damage, screen-wide control
items.notes: High Wizard discussions consistently frame the class around choosing between its main AoE spell identities.

items.best_for: Tanky frontline play with flexible PvE and PvP options
items.notes: Paladin is one of the clearest examples of a class with multiple viable skill identities instead of a single default path.

items.best_for: Magic DPS players who want either consistent casting or more experimental proc-based gameplay
items.notes: Professor build coverage currently focuses on three recognizable routes: Autocast, Psychic Wave, and SP-focused variants.

items.best_for: Players who want a Merchant branch with strong offensive identity
items.notes: Biochemist build videos consistently compare Acid Demonstration and Hellplant as the two headline directions.

items.best_for: Party support, safer leveling, utility-heavy progression
items.notes: High Priest coverage commonly splits between traditional full-support play and more damage-oriented PvE variants.

highlights: Ragnarok Origin Classic build coverage is strongest when each class route gets a clear role label.
Sniper, Lord Knight, Wizard, and Priest are especially easy for players to compare at a glance.
Showing the skill route, role, and tradeoff together makes the section more useful than a simple list.

classBuilds.title: Ragnarok Origin Classic Class Builds
classBuilds.subtitle: Players do not only search for skill trees. They also want a full build direction that combines role, farming comfort, power curve, and stat emphasis.
classBuilds.intro: The most useful homepage version of class builds is not an overlong theorycraft wall. It is a fast overview that tells players what each class archetype actually feels like, what kind of content it suits, and why someone would pick it over a competing job path. For Ragnarok Origin Classic, the strongest publicly documented archetypes already revolve around ranged farming, front-line melee burst, magic AoE, hybrid tank builds, and specialized late-job branches.

items.focus: Attack speed, auto-attack uptime, Falcon synergy
Trap setup, positional play, area denial
strengths: Useful for players who prefer setup gameplay

items.focus: Single-target burst, weapon scaling, aggressive boss pressure
Spell burst, area coverage, magic scaling

strengths: Can play tanky without feeling passive
Good for players who like survivability

items.focus: Magic scaling, proc interactions, specialized resource-based damage
best_for: Players who want flexible mage builds

items.focus: Skill-driven ranged damage with class-specific utility
best_for: Players who want Merchant progression with real damage presence
strengths: Good branch for players tired of basic support expectations

items.focus: Buffs, healing, sustain, optional PvE damage direction
strengths: Flexible enough to branch into more active PvE setups

highlights: A clean class-build section should tell players the role, the feel, and the payoff quickly.
The strongest ROOC archetypes are already easy to group by farming, bossing, PvP, and support value.
This section stays useful even when players are only comparing two or three favorite jobs.

gearProgression.title: Ragnarok Origin Classic Gear Progression
gearProgression.subtitle: Players often get stuck once raw leveling slows down and they are no longer sure what to refine, buy, or farm next.
gearProgression.intro: Ragnarok Origin Classic character power is built through several linked systems rather than a single equipment screen. Public beginner and progression guides already point to a consistent growth order: refine core gear efficiently, fill feather slots early, use pets to improve combat output, add cards for damage and utility, and spend shop currencies on long-term value instead of random upgrades.

items.details: Current progression guidance recommends starting with the weapon and core accessories before spreading resources too thin.
items.why_now: This is the most efficient early power jump.

items.details: After the first weapon and accessory upgrades, round out the rest of the equipment so the character is not overinvested in only one slot.
items.why_now: Balanced refine value improves overall consistency.

items.details: Once a stable +6 baseline is set, weapon and accessories become the main refine targets again.
items.why_now: The next meaningful damage spike usually comes from higher refine thresholds.

items.details: Advanced refine effects and stronger scaling become more relevant at higher thresholds.
items.why_now: This is one of the clearest long-term investment tracks for account strength.

items.details: Feathers provide major stat layers such as Attack, Defense, Damage Boost, and Damage Reduction.
items.why_now: A complete feather setup matters more early than over-upgrading one weak feather.

items.focus: Do not overinvest in low-tier feathers
items.details: Set effects are determined by the lowest-rarity feather equipped, so early efficiency comes from coverage first, deep investment later.
items.why_now: This avoids wasting resources on temporary pieces.

items.details: Pet growth in Classic is simplified around level and tier, and pets are positioned as a major help for early and midgame farming.
items.why_now: Pets add practical combat value without requiring a fully finished gear setup first.

items.focus: Prioritize damage and utility cards first
items.details: Cards are sourced from systems such as Monster Annihilation and shop exchange, and are commonly treated as an efficiency layer for farming and combat performance.
items.why_now: Cards improve build identity and help speed up routine content.

items.details: Gold Shop priority starts with feather selection boxes and refine materials; Social Shop prioritizes feathers, then gold, then Blue Star Coin; Pet Shop prioritizes Epic and Legendary fragments; Card Shop specifically highlights Tanuki Card as an early priority.
items.why_now: Correct shop spending keeps progression clean for F2P and light spenders.

highlights: Ragnarok Origin Classic gear growth works best when refine, feathers, pets, cards, and shops all move together.
Early efficiency comes from complete coverage, not from overinvesting in a single slot too soon.
Long-term value decisions matter more than chasing small short-term upgrades.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Ragnarok Origin Classic

questions.question: What should I do first in Ragnarok Origin Classic?
questions.answer: In Ragnarok Origin Classic, push the main quest first, take your first job at level 10, craft your class weapon at level 15, and start Daily Commissions at level 16.

questions.question: Which class is easiest in Ragnarok Origin Classic?
questions.answer: Ragnarok Origin Classic is easiest for most players on Archer or Mage if they want safe ranged farming, while Acolyte is the smoothest support path.

questions.question: How do I level fast in Ragnarok Origin Classic?
questions.answer: Ragnarok Origin Classic leveling is fastest when you stay quest-first, finish Daily Commissions, use combat time on efficient mobs, and avoid early elite detours.

questions.question: Where do I find Ragnarok Origin Classic codes?
questions.answer: Ragnarok Origin Classic codes are usually shared through official launch pages, Discord, and community posts. Keep the Codes module open and copy the current active entry.

questions.question: Is Ragnarok Origin Classic good for free-to-play players?
questions.answer: Yes. Ragnarok Origin Classic supports a steady F2P route if you focus on daily rewards, efficient gear upgrades, and value-first systems instead of cosmetic spending.

questions.question: What should I join first in Ragnarok Origin Classic: guild or PvP?
questions.answer: In Ragnarok Origin Classic, join a guild early, then branch into PvP, MVP hunting, and War of Emperium once your basic build is stable.
